fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the cash flow account.

fields.name: Search Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies an alternate name that you can use to search for the record in question when you cannot remember the value in the Name field.

fields.name: Account Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the purpose of the cash flow account. Newly created cash flow accounts are automatically assigned the Entry account type, but you can change this.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip: Specifies the comment for the record.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the related record is blocked from being posted in transactions, for example a customer that is declared insolvent or an item that is placed in quarantine.

fields.name: New Page
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you want a new page to start immediately after this cash flow account when you print the chart of cash flow accounts.

fields.name: No. of Blank Lines
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of blank lines that you want to be inserted before this cash flow account in the chart of cash flow accounts.

fields.name: Totaling
fields.tooltip: Specifies an account interval or a list of account numbers. The entries of the account will be totaled to give a total balance. How entries are totaled depends on the value in the Account Type field.

fields.name: Source Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the source type that applies to the source number that is shown in the Source No. field.

fields.name: G/L Integration
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the cash flow account has integration with the general ledger. When a cash flow account has integration with the general ledger, either the balances of the general ledger accounts or their budgeted values are used in the cash flow forecast.

fields.name: G/L Account Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies that only the cash flow entries that are registered to the filtered general ledger accounts are included in the cash flow forecast.
